rationale: control_dependence: failure -- C03-P33 (an execution error marks the node 'buggy' and ends that cycle), C03-P37 (VLM-flagged plot issues mark a node buggy and record feedback 'for future debugging'), C03-P40 ('a buggy node is chosen' with predefined probability, 'prioritizing error resolution and debugging'), C03-P42/P43/P44 (buggy parents and stage errors 'trigger the creation of corresponding debug nodes'), C03-P49 (VLM feedback 'is used to generate new experimental nodes ... aimed at addressing the identified issues'). score -- C03-P07 ('the best-performing checkpoint is selected to seed the next stage'), C03-P29 ('an LLM-based evaluator assesses all leaf nodes and selects the most promising one', 'effectively pruning less promising research avenues'), C03-P41 ('best-first search strategy guided by GPT-4o, which evaluates candidates based on ... performance metrics, training dynamics and ... plots'). observation_result -- C03-P27 (stage-1/2 stopping criteria are triggered by 'a basic working prototype ... successfully executed' and 'convergence in training curves'), C03-P04 (results are given back to the system 'to take notes ... for future planning'). No fixed_or_predefined/not_reported: every reported selection is conditioned on an error or performance signal, and the source states the dependence. selection_signal names the concrete drivers accordingly. selection_objective: local_repair -- the buggy-node debugging paths (C03-P33/P37/P40/P42/P43/P44/P49). performance_improvement -- node and checkpoint selection is explicitly keyed to performance metrics (C03-P29, C03-P41, C03-P07). This case is performance best-first, NOT uncertainty-directed: C03-P45 (replication nodes compute 'statistical measures (mean and s.d.)' that 'enhance the robustness of the results') is about the robustness of reported outcomes, not a selection signal -- no experiment is chosen to reduce posterior uncertainty -- so control_dependence carries no uncertainty value and selection_objective carries neither uncertainty_reduction nor hypothesis_model_discrimination. The former 'exploration' label splits out of selection_objective into generation_scope (below): the redundancy-avoidance in C03 is a generation-side diversification, not a selection purpose, so no diversity_directed_selection at the pi level (selection is best-first on performance). generation_scope: search_space_expansion -- the template-free tree search is built 'to overcome the limitations of a fixed starting codebase' for 'more open-ended discovery' (C03-P20), 'wider scientific exploration' (C03-P01), with abstract, code-untethered ideation (C03-P22/P23) grounded against the literature to 'identify knowledge gaps and avoid rediscovering existing work' (C03-P24). candidate_diversification -- hyperparameter and ablation nodes 'maintain records of previously tested' configurations 'to prevent redundant experiments'/'avoid repetition' (C03-P43/C03-P44), diversifying the generated experiment candidates. Compute budget and per-node runtime caps (C03-P27 'allocated computational budget is exhausted', C03-P28 'a maximum experiment runtime of 1 h') are termination constraints, noted here rather than encoded on any axis. candidate_selection_rule: top_k_ranked -- the LLM-based evaluator selects the most promising leaf and prunes less promising avenues (C03-P29), best-first search retains the highest-scoring candidates (C03-P41), the best checkpoint seeds the next stage (C03-P07), and 'the system selects several nodes ... to expand in parallel' (C03-P39); sampled_subset -- 'With a predefined probability, a buggy node is chosen; otherwise, a non-buggy node is selected' (C03-P40), a probabilistic (stochastic) node selection layered on the ranking. candidate_execution_rule: batch -- selected/child nodes 'are executed concurrently in parallel' (C03-P42); until_budget -- execution runs until the per-node runtime cap or the stage compute budget is exhausted (C03-P27/P28). No objective label appears in either rule. design_selection_rule: coverage_or_factorial -- the experimental design is chosen by systematic coverage of a configuration/ablation space: stage-2 hyperparameter nodes 'systematically explore alternative hyperparameter configurations' while 'maintain[ing] records of previously tested hyperparameters to prevent redundant experiments' (C03-P43), and stage-4 ablation nodes 'evaluate crucial ablation studies ... [assessing] the importance of various components or assumptions' with 'previously tested ablation conditions ... tracked to avoid repetition' (C03-P44); replication nodes add seed replicates for statistical measures (C03-P45). This is a coverage/factorial design of experiments (systematic sweep + ablation), not a discrimination- or information-directed selection of experiments to reduce posterior uncertainty about competing hypotheses; no discrimination-, information-, or cost-directed design selection is reported. formal_epistemic_utility_evidence: not_reported -- no proposition states a prior/posterior, likelihood, or EIG/epistemic-utility term used directly in node or checkpoint selection (corpus-level footnote).
